title: USDA Layer Format Open Contract

included: USDA text layer format recognition for generic layer-open dispatch
.usda resolved-extension fast-path participation for layer-open dispatch
UTF-8 decoding of opened resource bytes
canonical layer dump adapter pass-through only at validation boundaries
format-handler diagnostics before parser state is emitted

model.OpenedResourceBytes: Bytes or a file-backed byte view supplied by usd-layer-open after resource opening. This is not a USD payload composition arc.
model.FormatHandler: A USDA-only handler that either decodes and delegates to usda-spec-parser or reports a handler diagnostic.
model.LayerOpenResult: A target-native result containing either a document-model Layer or diagnostics. It is not a JSON object.
model.CanonicalDump: The layer-dump JSON shape used only by adapters and integration goldens.

input.displayName: resolved resource display spelling or extension metadata

output.accepted: true for USDA text layer candidates

input.bytes: opened resource bytes or an equivalent non-owning byte view

output.result: target-native LayerOpenResult containing a document-model Layer or diagnostics

laws: .usda resources are USDA layer candidates and may be selected by layer-open before content is read for USDA text-header inspection
.usdc and .usdz resources are not accepted by this handler
resource I/O and unsupported resource diagnostics are not owned by this handler
when selected by resolved extension, the handler validates opened bytes as USDA text and must not redispatch based on opened byte content
opened resource bytes must be decoded as UTF-8 before parser handoff
USDA grammar parsing must be delegated to usda-spec-parser
open_usda_bytes and open_usda_text must not return Json, dict, tagged JSON, or canonical dump trees as their domain result
opened resource bytes must not be described as payloads in this contract; payload is reserved for USD payload composition arcs
implementations should avoid copying the entire opened resource when a safe byte span, string view, memory map, or equivalent view can be handed to the parser
JSON materialization is allowed only in explicit adapters, diagnostics serialization, and dump commands
the handler must not perform resource protocol classification or file URI conversion
the handler must not mutate document-model storage directly

the handler must accept opened resource bytes from layer-open; invoking usd-resource-protocol reads (read_resource, read_binary_file, read_text_file_or_empty, or equivalent) directly with a raw identifier string is a contract violation
non-UTF-8 opened bytes fail with diagnostics before partial layer output
empty opened resources produce a successful empty Layer result, not a ResourceOpenFailed diagnostic; open-failure is reported by layer-open before bytes ever reach the handler

when file_backed_path is present on the opened resource, the handler must forward it to usda-spec-parser so future file-backed strategies can compose; copying provenance into an unstructured string parameter is not sufficient
valid USDA bytes are parsed through usda-spec-parser
canonical dump output must be obtained through an explicit adapter; returning canned dump JSON selected by file content or display spelling is a contract violation
this contract must not call USDC parsers or USDZ package readers

adapter_protocol.input: Future unit adapters may accept a JSON case with opened resource bytes or text.
adapter_protocol.output: Adapters convert LayerOpenResult to the ok, diagnostics, and layer JSON shape in contracts/layer-dump.schema.json.
